message:
fix(lab): fix scheduler lock scope and enhance task execution tracing

Two related fixes to the lab runtime's task scheduling and execution:

src/plan/fixtures.rs — Fix lock scope in execute_plan_in_lab_core:
- The scheduler mutex was held across the println!("Scheduled tasks!")
  call, which is unnecessary and could introduce contention. Restructured
  to drop the lock immediately after scheduling completes, capturing
  is_empty into a local variable for the subsequent log line.
- Changed initial task priority from hardcoded 0 to 255, matching the
  pattern used elsewhere for initial scheduling at maximum priority.
- In the retry/reschedule loop, replaced hardcoded priority 0 with
  record.sched_priority so rescheduled tasks respect their assigned
  priority rather than all running at the same level.
- Minor formatting: broke long println into multi-line for readability.

src/lab/runtime.rs — Enhance debug logging:
- Added rng_value and priority fields to the task execution trace
  (the println that fires for the first 100 steps). This makes it
  possible to diagnose scheduling order issues by seeing the actual
  random values and priorities that determined execution order.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
